content: Just as memory-efficient Intrinsic Curiosity Module (ICM) implementations prune low-value novelty signals to avoid overwhelming large-scale autonomous agents' memory budgets, modular LLM-based AI-agent frameworks can embed a lightweight ICM-inspired gating layer to prune low-value tool call traces and historical interaction fragments that bloat agent context and degrade composability. This turns the problem of LLM agent context overcrowding into an intrinsic curiosity-guided sparsification problem, rather than a purely semantic summarization problem.
source_topics: Memory-efficient ICM implementation for large-scale autonomous agents
